name: Lithocholic acid reproduced calorie-restriction-like AMPK and animal healthspan signals
summary: Lithocholic acid adds a high-profile preclinical bile-acid metabolite signal, with safety and taxonomy caveats.
statement: Lithocholic acid, identified as a calorie-restriction-associated metabolite, activated AMPK, enhanced mouse muscle regeneration, grip strength, and running capacity, and induced life-extending or health-extending effects in C. elegans and Drosophila through AMPK-dependent biology.
population_or_model: Mouse muscle-function and regeneration models plus C. elegans and Drosophila aging models
time_horizon: Model-specific calorie-restriction and lifespan or healthspan assay windows
quantitative_note: The abstract reports AMPK-dependent anti-aging phenotypes but does not provide a single effect size suitable for this staged finding.
caveats: This is preclinical evidence, not human metabolite restoration.
Lithocholic acid is a secondary bile acid with plausible chronic-safety concerns.
The biology overlaps calorie-restriction mimetics and AMPK modulation, not only dysbiosis.
